name: Ramp Corporate Card MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Ramp — the corporate card and expense management platform. Enables AI agents to query Ramp transactions, expense reports, budgets, and card data. Allows finance automation agents to retrieve spend analytics, flag policy violations, and generate expense summaries.
use_cases: Querying Ramp transactions and spend data from finance automation agents
Generating expense reports and spend summaries from financial reporting agents
Checking budget utilization and flagging overspend from budget management agents
Retrieving card and employee spend data for accounting workflow agents
not_for: Creating or approving expense reports autonomously (requires human approval)
Personal expense management (Ramp is corporate only)
best_when: A finance team using Ramp wants AI agents to query spend data, generate expense summaries, and flag budget issues — reducing manual finance operations.
avoid_when: You need to approve expenses or issue cards autonomously. FINANCIAL RISK: Corporate spend data is sensitive; autonomous expense approval requires human oversight.

auth.notes: Ramp OAuth 2.0. Requires Ramp developer account. Use read-only scopes for reporting agents. Write scopes for expense submission automation require strict governance.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Ramp is free for businesses; API access included
pricing.notes: Ramp corporate card platform is free. API access included with Ramp account. MCP server is community open source.

performance.rate_limits: Ramp API: rate limits apply per account; documented in Ramp developer docs

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Corporate spend data and card controls — requires strict access governance
Expense approval workflows require human in the loop — never approve autonomously
Community MCP implementation — verify against current Ramp API v1 documentation
Card issuance and limit changes require human authorization
Ramp is US-only corporate card platform
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Corporate spend data. SOC2 and PCI-DSS certified. OAuth scopes essential. Read-only for reporting, write for submission automation requires governance.

security_score_components.security_notes: Corporate financial data. SOC2 certified. PCI-DSS compliant. OAuth required. Card control operations require human oversight.
